arts.ttl: Blackburn Rovers identify Lewis Baker replacement as first January deal edges closer
arts.sum: Blackburn Rovers are closing in on a breakthrough to bolster John Eustace's squad

arts.ttl: Isaiah Jones's Middlesbrough exit confirmed ending incredible non-league fairytale
arts.sum: Isaiah Jones's football fairytale story at Middlesbrough has come to an end as his four-and-a-half year stay concluded with confirmation of his transfer to Luton Town. The transfer ends a remarkable story for the winger at Boro, having been plucked from non-league obscurity to quickly become a Championship break-out star.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard responds to Blackburn Rovers transfer interest in Coventry man
arts.sum: The Coventry City boss was asked about Blackburn Rovers' interest in defender Liam Kitching

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough predicted line-up for Blackburn with many changes anticipated in the FA Cup - Craig Johns
arts.sum: But we expect in the absence of other senior options, that Hackney and Barlaser will start the game. The Finn has enjoyed a good week of training ahead of this one, but won't be expected to start the game.

arts.ttl: Soul-searching and an arm from Eustace - Harry Leonard's Blackburn Rovers comeback
arts.sum: The Blackburn Rovers striker is back from injury and could make his first start for more than 12 months against Middlesbrough

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough Mark Travers January transfer truth as goalkeeper situation assessed
arts.sum: That makes signing a keeper this month a possibility for Boro and Travers is an option being explored, as Bournemouth appear likely to allow the 25-year-old to leave on loan. That said, Boro can be patient in assessing their options because the Cherries won't be able to let Travers leave until after next Tuesday's clash with Chelsea because first-choice keeper Kepa Arrizabalaga is on loan from the Blues and therefore ineligible for that game.

arts.ttl: John Eustace interest poses ultimate question at Blackburn Rovers board
arts.sum: Ian Herbert returns with his latest column, reflecting on the derby defeat and John Eustace's hopes for January reinforcements

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough transfer stance unchanged despite no Emmanuel Latte Lath guarantees
arts.sum: Michael Carrick wasn't able to offer any assurances that Middlesbrough can keep Emmanuel Latte Lath this month, but said their goal for the transfer window - to finish it stronger than they started - remains unchanged. Asked if he was confident Boro could keep him this month, particularly with the business side of a potential sale in mind, Carrick said: “You never know what’s out there and what’s going to come - who’s going to offer what.
